summary: Iteration 47 backend testing complete — Department-prefixed numbering, Department Master CRUD, and expanded visibility scoping. 18 of 19 pytest cases pass; 1 RFQ test skipped (requires approved-PR + vendor chain; the same stamp_dept_doc utility is already validated through PR + 5 other endpoints). Verified: (a) sequences.next_dept_doc + stamp_dept_doc generate HR/ADV/2026/#### style IDs and increment atomically across calls; (b) advance create stamps BOTH legacy advance_no (AD-####) AND new dept_doc_no + ownership_department='hr'; (c) PR, DPR, Enquiry, payment_in all stamp the correct PRO/PR, OPS/DPR, SAL/ENQ, FIN/RCT prefixes with the correct ownership_department; (d) Department Master CRUD: 9 seed slugs returned, non-admin (project_manager) gets 403 on POST, duplicate slug gets 409, items endpoint accepts sub_departments/branches/business_units and rejects invalid kind with 400, built-in slug delete is blocked with 400, custom dept can be deleted; (e) scope expansion: project_manager sees ≤ super_admin counts on employees, employee_advances/advances, and all six project-scoped collections (purchase_requisitions/rfqs/grns/dprs/measurements/ra_bills).

minor.issue: PUT update does not enforce slug uniqueness — an admin can update an existing custom dept to collide with another dept's slug (only POST checks duplicates). Suggest adding the same find_one({'slug': new_slug, 'id': {'$ne': dept_id}}) guard before $set.

Built-in protection compares against the in-memory DEFAULT_DEPARTMENTS list. If a built-in slug is renamed via PUT (since slug is mutable) the protection silently bypasses. Either freeze slug on built-ins or store an is_builtin flag in the seed doc.
No de-dup beyond Mongo $addToSet (which is fine), but kind validation is duplicated across POST and DELETE handlers — extract into a constant set.

action_items: (Optional/low) Add slug-uniqueness guard to PUT /admin/department-master/{id} so admins cannot collide with another dept's slug.
(Optional/low) Mark seeded built-in departments with `is_builtin: true` so renaming the slug does not bypass the delete-block.

(Optional/low) When RFQ create is exercised in a future iteration, seed an approved PR + vendor in a fixture so the dept_doc_no PRO/RFQ stamping can be asserted end-to-end (currently skipped due to non-trivial chain).
critical_code_review_comments: advance_router.py remains 974+ lines (flagged in iter46 already) — Phase A/B/C/D/E and now Iter47 dept stamping should be split into sub-modules.

stamp_dept_doc unconditionally calls .setdefault('ownership_department', ...) — fine for new docs, but make sure no caller is overriding ownership_department to None (would skip the default). Currently safe because no caller pre-sets it to None, but a one-line guard `if owner: doc.setdefault(...)` would be defensive.

DEPT_DOC_MAP in sequences.py duplicates the dept→code mapping that also lives in department_master_router.DEFAULT_DEPARTMENTS (e.g. HR, PRO, SAL, ACC, FIN, OPS, SAF, LOG, STO). If an admin renames a dept code via PUT, dept_doc_no prefixes still come from the hardcoded map — they will drift. Consider deriving DEPT_DOC_MAP at startup from db.department_master.

crud_router._list_filter merges project + dept filters with $and — correct, but when a user has BOTH project_filter and department_filter sentinels=false (i.e., no scope override needed), the function still constructs a list with {} as the first element, which Mongo handles but is mildly wasteful. Skip the $and wrap when one side is empty {}.

department_master_router.py: list_departments has no `active` filter — clients receive inactive departments mixed with active ones. Either accept an `?active=true` query or always filter active=True with an admin override.
test_credentials: admin@erp.com/Admin@123 (super_admin), hr.test@erp.com/HR@12345 (hr_executive), test_pm@erp.com/PM@12345 (project_manager), purchase@erp.com/Purchase@123 (purchase_officer), sales@erp.com/Sales@123 (sales_executive) — all 5 logged in successfully against the preview backend.
seed_data_creation: Created: 1 TEST_ employee in dept=hr; 2 new advances (HR/ADV/2026/#### counter verified strictly increment by 1); 1 PR (PRO/PR/2026/####); 1 enquiry (SAL/ENQ/2026/####) for 'TEST47 Client …'; 1 DPR (OPS/DPR/2026/####) on first available project; 1 payment_in (FIN/RCT/2026/####, ₹1000 cash, client_name TEST47); 1 custom department-master row (slug test47_<rand>) that was created → PUT-renamed → custom-deleted in-test (cleaned up). No built-in department modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter47 dept-prefix numbering + dept master CRUD + expanded scoping is stable. To regression-test next iteration just run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration47_dept_scope.py -v`. The RFQ stamping skip is intentional — to cover it you need to (a) create a PR, (b) approve it through the procurement approval chain as super_admin, (c) seed a vendor, then (d) POST /api/procurement/rfqs with {pr_id, vendors:[{vendor_id}]}. The stamp_dept_doc('rfq') call at procurement_router.py:300 is identical to the working PR/PO/GRN ones, so behaviour is highly likely correct.
rca of the issue: No production bugs found. All Iter47 acceptance criteria pass: department-prefixed numbering atomically increments per (dept, type, year) tuple; legacy numbering (advance_no, pr_number, etc.) remains untouched; visibility scoping correctly expanded (project_manager <= super_admin counts on all newly-scoped collections); department-master endpoints enforce RBAC (403 for non-admins) and integrity (409 on duplicate slug, 400 on built-in delete, 400 on invalid item kind).
